title: Hemionitis arequipensis
taxonomy: Hemionitis arequipensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This family is commonly known as the polypody ferns. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Hemionitis arequipensis is further classified into the genus Hemionitis. The genus Hemionitis is a small group of ferns with mostly tropical and subtropical distributions. Further classification would place Hemionitis arequipensis within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida.

intro: Hemionitis arequipensis, also known as Arequipa chain fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis arequipensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and ointments to treat various health conditions.
